citations.narrative:
An APHIS official observed 8 rabbits housed in the same primary enclosure as one Indian peafowl. Sharing an enclosure
with other species can pose a health and safety risk for the rabbits, such as infectious diseases or injuries from
interaction.
Rabbits may not be housed in the same primary enclosure with any other species unless required for scientific reasons.
The facility must ensure that animals housed in the same primary enclosure are maintained in compatible groups. Correct
by June 10, 2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative.
n

narrative:
3.58(a) 
   
Classification and separation. 
An APHIS official observed 8 rabbits housed in the same primary enclosure as one Indian peafowl. Sharing an enclosure 
with other species can pose a health and safety risk for the rabbits, such as infectious diseases or injuries from 
interaction.  
 
Rabbits may not be housed in the same primary enclosure with any other species unless required for scientific reasons. 
The facility must ensure that animals housed in the same primary enclosure are maintained in compatible groups. Correct 
by June 10, 2026. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative. 
 
 n
